published_prior_art.cite: HemerikGoeman2018, WangLiYu2026, and DobribanYu2025 supply adjacent group-invariance or causal-prediction machinery, not a universal two-arm observable-selector orbit-mass iff.
RamdasBarberCandesTibshirani2023 concerns randomized permutation references; convex-hull/LP feasibility is standard, while the common-target two-arm application is distinct.

TibshiraniBarberCandesRamdas2019 gives the weighted-exchangeability likelihood-ratio and infinity-augmented quantile construction; the orbit-selector specialization is distinct but not a new generic weighting principle.
BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 already studies conformal coverage degradation beyond exchangeability; the finite orbit-mass reduction is a specialized certificate.
The k=M+1 infinite-threshold conclusion is immediate from standard split-conformal rank inversion.
Support mismatch is a known weighted-conformal failure mode; this is a useful nondegenerate two-arm orbit witness.
BaiLiuShaikhTabordMeehan2024 supplies the matched-pair assignment representation; the proposed reduction is an algebraic consequence in the new selector framework.
A concrete unit test of the proposed construction, rather than a separately broad published-theorem advance.

DobribanYu2025 and BarberCandesRamdasTibshirani2023 treat symmetry-based prediction and nonexchangeability robustness, but neither checked source gives this common-target two-arm finite orbit frontier. JinRen2024 is relevant unpublished selection-conditional conformal WIP, not a published subsuming theorem.

novelty_flags.label: WangLiYu2026 bibliography and related-work anchor
novelty_flags.one_line: The core represents WangLiYu2026 as a 2026 Statistica Sinica publication, but the journal’s own page identifies it as preprint SS-2025-0476; correct its source status and anchor any pair-matching comparison to inspectable theorem text.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: All finite-coverage statements
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Coverage is conditional on iid replicated matched-block superpopulation arrays, external independent training, prespecified target selection, and auxiliary selector draws; it is not a design-based guarantee for the single Shamba Maisha finite population.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The weighted and unweighted coverage claims are accepted only in this replicated-array conditional regime.

source_verification_receipts.version: Statistica Sinica preprint SS-2025-0476, not a published journal version
source_verification_receipts.verified_claim: The official page labels the item “Preprint No. SS-2025-0476” and describes finite-sample conformal cluster and individual treatment-effect prediction intervals with arbitrary working outcome models.
The article exists as Exact testing with random permutations and concerns exact random-permutation methodology under group-based permutation arguments.
The paper gives weighted conformal prediction under known or estimable test-to-training likelihood ratios and develops its weighted-exchangeability formulation.

For completely randomized or stratified randomized experiments with perfect compliance, the paper gives finite-sample average coverage for counterfactual and individual-treatment-effect intervals, including weighted conformal treatment of nonconstant known propensities.
Theorem 2.2 validates randomized permutation p-values when permutations are sampled from an arbitrary distribution.
The paper studies robustness of conformal coverage under failures of exchangeability, using weighted quantiles and randomization techniques.
SymmPI gives predictive inference under general group symmetries, proves coverage under distributional invariance, and characterizes distribution-shift effects.
The paper defines matched-pair cluster randomization as pairing clusters and randomly selecting one cluster in each pair for treatment, and studies associated inference and randomization tests.
The Shamba Maisha study is a cluster randomized clinical trial of the named multisectoral agricultural intervention in Kenya.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the WangLiYu2026 journal citation with its official preprint status unless a final version becomes available; then add a page/theorem anchor for the precise simple-randomization and pair-matching comparison.

Add a strict comparison proposition identifying exactly when the LeiCandès2021 unit-level weighted construction agrees with the orbit-selector construction and exhibit a matched-pair regime where it cannot implement the common-target selector.

Turn the frontier conjecture into an explicit finite optimization algorithm for pair blocks, with input size, constraints, and a worked nontrivial two-orbit calculation beyond the zero-coverage boundary.

recommended_next_step: Correct and re-anchor the WangLiYu comparator, then advance the field-tier frontier kernel to Stage 0.
